news.title: Berkshire Hathaway begins repurchasing shares, CEO Greg Abel buys $15 million in stock
news.summary: The 62-year-old executive's purchase came a little more than two months into his tenure running the Omaha-based conglomerate. The 62- year-old exec's purchase comes a little over two months after he took over the company.

news.title: Iran war threatens $11.7 trillion global travel industry as passengers get caught in crossfire
news.summary: Travelers who are far from falling missiles, drone attacks and other geopolitical flashpoints aren't immune to ripple effects. The U.S. has been involved in more than a dozen drone attacks in recent years.

news.title: Merkley proposes prediction market ban for government officials after Maduro, Iran bets
news.summary: Lawmakers are looking to ban insider trading on prediction markets. After a series of dubious bets placed on the ouster of Venezuela's Maduro and Iran strikes, lawmakers want to ban it.

news.title: Spain rejects White House claim it agreed to cooperate with U.S. forces amid Iran war
news.summary: The White House says Spain will now cooperate with the U.S. militarily over the Iran conflict. Madrid swiftly rejected this claim and said it would not join the war effort.

news.title: Lawmakers to introduce bill strengthening federal anti-poverty program: It's 'a critical lifeline,' Warren says
news.summary: SSI was created in 1972 to help the nation's most vulnerable. Some features of the program have not been updated in decades.

news.title: This stock-market correction signal just triggered for only the third time in seven years. Here’s the message for investors.
news.summary: Variant Perception said its S&P 500 “Correction Signal” has only been triggered three times since 2019. Variant Perception: S&s;P500 “ correction signal’s only triggered three time since 2019, not all at once.

news.title: Jobless claims and Challenger Gray point to declining layoffs. What about hiring plans?
news.summary: The number of people who lost jobs and applied for unemployment benefits at the end of February clung to recent lows. It added to a flurry of signals suggesting the labor market has stabilized.

news.title: ‘When he doesn’t get money, he becomes angry’: My brother has led a life of chaos and financial ruin. What is my moral obligation?
news.summary: “Without clear distance and boundaries, he will pull me down with him,” she says. “Withoutclear distance and boundary, he’s going to pull you down too.”

news.title: I walked away from $70 million in sales to remake my brand. Shrinking the business actually made it more profitable.
news.summary: Tibi founder Amy Smilovic talks about building the fashion retail giant — her way. She also answers the question every successful entrepreneur faces.
